submitted_payload.description: Ralph Workflow is a free and open-source composable loop framework and AI orchestrator for developers doing ambitious repo work. It composes planning, implementation, and verification into a reviewable workflow so unattended coding runs end in finished, tested code instead of another confident summary.
submitted_payload.tags: coding, autonomous coding, claude code, codex, workflow orchestration, open source

why_this_action: Shared artifacts still show distribution_and_message_to_primary_repo_conversion as the live bottleneck, Apollo is already in a measurement window until 2026-05-30, Reddit remains structurally blocked/high-risk, and OpenAgents.pro exposed a fresh coding-category submission lane with a live form endpoint that can route evaluators directly to the primary Codeberg repo.
shared_findings_reused: MARKETING_WORKFLOW_PRINCIPLES.md: prioritize Codeberg-first distribution and proof-led positioning
FOUR_MARKETING_QUESTIONS.md: preserved what it is, who it is for, why different, why now
ADOPTION_FUNNEL_NEXT.md: bias toward free-use path and proof-led conversion instead of more generic posting
market_intelligence_latest.json: reused positioning truths and competitor-aware framing
channel_discovery.json: existing easy-submit lanes are largely exhausted or already in measurement, so a fresh executable directory lane is higher leverage

measurement_window.success_signal: live OpenAgents.pro listing or backlink pointing evaluators to the Codeberg primary repo
measurement_window.kill_condition: If no listing or reply signal arrives by 2026-06-06, replace this lane with a different untouched curator/directory surface instead of resubmitting the same pitch.
